articles.title: Airwallex Research Pegs Cross-Border B2B Payment Friction at $330bn
articles.summary: Airwallex and the Centre for Economics and Business Research published analysis quantifying a 'Global Growth Tariff' of roughly $330 billion in working capital tied up by legacy B2B cross-border payment systems, with EMEA bearing about $144 billion. For a product lawyer this is a market-positioning and advertising-substantiation matter that supports Airwallex's policy case for faster, more transparent settlement infrastructure.

articles.title: Revolut Eyes US Bank Launch With FDIC-Insured Accounts and Stablecoins
articles.summary: Reporting confirmed Revolut is pursuing a US national bank charter (filed March) to launch a branchless Stamford-based bank around 2027, bundling FDIC-insured accounts with stablecoin, crypto and multi-currency deposits aimed at cross-border customers. For Airwallex this is a competitive signal underscoring the strategic premium on US licensing depth and the convergence of stablecoin rails with mainstream banking.

articles.title: MAS Revokes Major Payment Institution Licence of Bsquared Technology
articles.summary: MAS revoked Bsquared Technology's Major Payment Institution licence effective May 14, 2026 after an inspection found serious risk-management and outsourcing failings and false or misleading information provided from application through inspection. For Airwallex, which holds MAS licensing and is co-headquartered in Singapore, this is a precedent on outsourcing, related-party governance and candour with the regulator.

articles.title: HKMA Grants First Two Stablecoin Licences to Anchorpoint and HSBC
articles.summary: The HKMA granted Hong Kong's first stablecoin issuer licences to Anchorpoint (Standard Chartered/HKT/Animoca JV) and HSBC, approving only about 5.6% of 36 applicants and favouring bank-led issuers with strong AML and reserve credentials. Both plan HKD-referenced stablecoins for cross-border payments, a use case directly adjacent to Airwallex's core business and a likely template for other APAC regulators.

articles.title: AUSTRAC Orders External AML/CTF Audit of Airwallex
articles.summary: AUSTRAC ordered a section 162 external audit of Airwallex Designated Business Group citing suspected serious non-compliance, flagging transaction monitoring not attuned to multi-jurisdictional risk and weak suspicious-matter reporting and oversight, with findings due within 180 days at Airwallex's expense. This is the most directly material item for Airwallex, carrying reputational, IPO-timing and potential civil-penalty exposure as broader AUSTRAC enforcement intensifies.

articles.title: FCA Safeguarding Supplementary Regime Takes Effect 7 May 2026
articles.summary: The FCA's PS25/12 CASS 15 regime came into force on May 7, 2026, imposing daily reconciliations, a monthly safeguarding return, annual safeguarding audits, resolution packs and standardised acknowledgement letters, with third-party appointments to be reviewed within three months. For Airwallex's UK-regulated entities this is a hard deadline driving material operational, governance, audit and reporting change with real enforcement risk.

articles.title: US Treasury Endorses Revised FATF Cross-Border Payments Standard
articles.summary: Treasury endorsed FATF's revised payments standard, which explicitly recognises non-bank financial institutions, promotes transparency and ties into updated Recommendation 16 Travel Rule requirements for ISO 20022-aligned data on transfers above USD/EUR 1,000, effective no later than 2030. For Airwallex as a non-bank cross-border PSP, the recognition of non-bank PSPs and the data-transparency direction directly shape future AML compliance architecture.

articles.title: ASIC Digital Assets Roadmap; INFO 225 No-Action Relief Expires June 2026
articles.summary: ASIC released an 18-month roadmap for the Digital Assets Framework Act (Royal Assent April 8, 2026; commencing April 9, 2027), with licence applications opening in months 12–18 and the INFO 225 class no-action position expiring in June 2026. For Melbourne-headquartered Airwallex this clarifies Australia's trajectory for any digital-asset or stablecoin-adjacent product and sets a near-term deadline to lodge applications or ensure existing-licence compliance.

articles.title: Airwallex Secures Full Commercial Licensing in Malaysia from Bank Negara
articles.summary: Airwallex received e-money issuing and Class A licences from Bank Negara Malaysia, adding to its Class B Money Services Business licence and Registered Merchant Acquirer status to enable a full suite of payment services, after processing over RM2 billion in 2025 remittance volume. This is a positive licensing milestone expanding Airwallex's regulated APAC footprint and signalling deepening regulatory trust as it builds full-stack non-bank coverage.

articles.title: Revolut Wins Full UK Banking Licence; Wise Pays $4.2m US MSB Penalty
articles.summary: Revolut Bank UK exited mobilisation on March 11, 2026 with full PRA approval granting FSCS protection to 13 million UK users, while Wise earlier paid a $4.2 million Multi-State MSB Examination Taskforce penalty and now holds 70+ licences globally. These competitor developments highlight diverging regulatory regimes (banking deposit protection versus EMI safeguarding) and ongoing enforcement exposure relevant to Airwallex's own licensing strategy and US compliance posture.
